title: Prompt Lifecycle, Monitoring, and Production Capstone · Prompt Lens: Prompt Registry
summary: Ship and maintain a cross-provider prompt system with regression evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt prompt registry increment in a production prompt portfolio and release gate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Store owners, versions, model assumptions, templates, and evaluation links in a prompt registry.
Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Prompt Registry
steps.instruction:

steps.title: Build Prompt Registry evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-prompt-registry-1egvnv" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Prompt Registry into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-prompt-registry-1egvnv
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

stimulus.title: Prompt Registry failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Prompt text can be edited in production without traceability.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Store owners, versions, model assumptions, templates, and evaluation links in a prompt registry.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Prompt Registry, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the Prompt Registry model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Prompt Registry applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Store owners, versions, model assumptions, templates, and evaluation links in a prompt registry.
